title: Denver Polyiso Envelope CI Checklist
lens: Evidence-first preconstruction and submittal risk control

decision_gates.question: Do submitted polyiso assemblies map to an approved NFPA 285 tested configuration?
decision_gates.pass_condition: Exact assembly match or approved engineering judgment with signed manufacturer letter.
decision_gates.fail_action: Block release for procurement and trigger alternate assembly review.

decision_gates.question: Are submittals complete with ICC-ES reports, flame spread, smoke developed, and thermal data?
decision_gates.pass_condition: All listed evidence is complete and current.
decision_gates.fail_action: Return submittal as incomplete and hold schedule commitments.

decision_gates.question: Does VE proposal preserve code compliance, warranty continuity, and attachment engineering?
decision_gates.pass_condition: VE package includes sealed analysis and no downgrade in required ratings.
decision_gates.fail_action: Reject VE option and document basis in bidder bulletin.

nfpa_285_matrix.assembly_family: Metal composite panel over polyiso on steel stud backup
nfpa_285_matrix.test_reference: NFPA 285 test report with matching cladding, insulation thickness, and WRB
accepted_substitutions: Equivalent polyiso product from same manufacturer family with identical facer and density
WRB substitution only when listed in tested assembly engineering judgment
nfpa_285_matrix.trigger: Any change in cladding type, insulation thickness, or attachment pattern

nfpa_285_matrix.assembly_family: Fiber cement rainscreen with cavity insulation and continuous polyiso
nfpa_285_matrix.test_reference: NFPA 285 listing and manufacturer engineering judgment package
accepted_substitutions: Fastener and clip substitutions with stamped thermal and structural impact memo
nfpa_285_matrix.trigger: Introduction of alternate clip system or cavity depth

sections.title: Evidence Intake and Document Control
sections.objective: Ensure every compliance decision is based on traceable, current evidence.

checks.prompt: Confirm latest drawing set, spec sections, and addenda are logged.
required_evidence: Spec section 07 thermal and moisture protection
checks.decision_logic: Pass only when all documents are version-aligned and dated within active bid period.

checks.prompt: Validate manufacturer technical data sheets and installation instructions are current.
checks.decision_logic: Fail if any technical document is superseded or product code mismatches submittal.

sections.title: NFPA 285 and Code Matrix Verification
sections.objective: Prevent non-compliant exterior wall assemblies from entering procurement.

checks.prompt: Map each wall type to NFPA 285 tested assembly lineage.
checks.decision_logic: Pass only when each wall type has direct traceability to tested assembly or accepted engineering judgment.

checks.prompt: Verify Denver and IBC amendment triggers for fire performance and combustibility.
checks.decision_logic: Fail if local amendment requirements are unaddressed in basis-of-design or substitutions.

sections.title: Submittal Completeness Checkpoints
sections.objective: Enforce first-pass completeness to avoid schedule and procurement churn.

checks.prompt: Confirm all product options include ESR listing, ASTM test data, and thermal values.
checks.decision_logic: Pass only if every proposed option includes complete code and performance documents.

checks.prompt: Verify accessory compatibility (adhesives, fasteners, air barrier interfaces).
checks.decision_logic: Fail when any interface lacks explicit compatibility approval.

sections.title: VE Defense and Risk Positioning
sections.objective: Screen VE proposals for hidden compliance, warranty, and constructability regressions.

checks.prompt: Quantify VE scope delta against baseline assembly and code obligations.
checks.decision_logic: Pass when VE demonstrates equal-or-better compliance and no unresolved risk transfer.

checks.prompt: Confirm VE alternatives preserve warranty chain and installer qualifications.
checks.decision_logic: Fail if warranty duration, coverage scope, or approved installer status is reduced.
